content.text: Answer four problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that a prime \(p \in \mathbb{N}\) such that \(p \equiv 3 \pmod 4\) is prime when considered as an element in the ring of Gaussian integers \(\mathbb{Z}[i]\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that a prime \(p \in \mathbb{N}\) such that \(p \equiv 1 \pmod 4\) is a product of two non-associate primes in \(\mathbb{Z}[i]\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Give a factorization of \(30\) as the product of a unit and powers of non-associate primes in \(\mathbb{Z}[i]\).

body.text: Let \(R\) be the ring of \(n \times n\) matrices over a field \(F\), where \(n \geq 2\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that \(R\) has no ideals other than \(0\) and \(R\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Exhibit a nonzero proper left ideal of \(R\).

body.text: Find one representative of each conjugacy class of elements of order \(4\) in the group \(GL(6,\mathbb{Q})\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: What does it mean to say that a field extension of \(F\) is algebraic?

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Let \(F \subset K \subset L\) be three fields. Prove that if \(K\) is algebraic over \(F\) and \(L\) is algebraic over \(K\), then \(L\) is algebraic over \(F\).

body.text: A commutative ring \(R\) with \(1\) is called a local ring if and only if it has a unique maximal ideal. Suppose that \(R\) is a ring with a proper ideal \(M\). Show that \(R\) is local, with maximal ideal \(M\), if and only if every element of \(R \setminus M\) (set difference) is a unit.
